title: App Router fundamentals
summary: The App Router uses React Server Components by default, nested layouts in layout.tsx, and file-based routes in the app directory.
content:
## Server vs Client

Server Components run on the server and ship minimal JS. Add `"use client"` when you need hooks, browser APIs, or event handlers.

## Routing

`page.tsx` defines a route segment. `layout.tsx` wraps nested segments. `loading.tsx` and `error.tsx` add UX boundaries.

## Data

Fetch in Server Components or use Route Handlers in `route.ts` for APIs.

code_examples.title: layout.tsx sketch

related_questions.preview: When do you mark a component as client?
Streaming and Suspense in App Router

title: Server Actions
summary: Server Actions are async functions that run on the server and can be invoked from forms or client code—useful for mutations without a separate API route.
content:
## Security

Treat Server Actions like endpoints: validate input, authorize users, and never trust client-supplied IDs alone.

## Progressive enhancement

Forms can post without JavaScript; enhance with client components as needed.
